title: ranunculus nemorosus subsp. timbalii
intro: Ranunculus nemorosus subsp. timbalii, also known as the Lesser Celandine, is a perennial plant in the buttercup family that grows in woodland areas with moist soil. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids and glycosides. The medicinal properties of Ranunculus nemorosus subsp. timbalii are said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
